methodology_note: NAD has the most transparent tithe flow data. For non-NAD divisions, the GC Working Policy sets the division→GC percentage (currently 2%, moving to 3% by 2030), but the conference→union→division split is set by each division's own working policy and is generally not published publicly. The GC Stewardship department states conferences worldwide retain 70-89% of tithe. Ranges are provided where exact figures are unavailable.

gc_tithe_parity_plan.description: Annual Council 2019 voted to achieve tithe parity among all 13 divisions by 2030. All divisions will contribute 3% of allocated tithe to the GC.

divisions.name: North American Division
divisions.special_funds: Defined benefit retirement fund (~11.4%), Conference Special Assistance Fund (~1%). After backflow of appropriations, conferences effectively benefit from ~83% of tithe dollar.
divisions.notes: Most transparent division. 2023 figures from NAD treasurer article. GC contribution declining from 5.85% toward 3% by 2030. The $100 breakdown: $66 conference, $10 retirement, $9 union, $9.15 NAD, $5.85 GC (2018 figures showed $66; 2023 article says 65.1%).

divisions.name: South Pacific Division
divisions.special_funds: SPD historically carries unique responsibility for Pacific mission territories (PNG, Pacific Islands) funded from its own resources rather than GC missionaries. Retirement costs are included within the conference/union/division percentages rather than as a separate line item as in NAD.
divisions.source: SNSW Conference known split (provided by stakeholder); SPD ESDA encyclopedia article confirms unique Pacific mission funding arrangement
divisions.notes: SNSW-specific split: ~80% conference, ~8% AUC (Australian Union Conference), ~10% SPD, ~2.4% GC. Other conferences within SPD (e.g., Greater Sydney, Victorian, South Queensland) may have slightly different splits. PNG and Pacific Island missions likely have different arrangements. The ~77% figure cited earlier likely accounts for retirement/other deductions from the 79.6% gross retention.

divisions.name: East-Central Africa Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available. GC Stewardship indicates conferences worldwide retain 70-89%.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions); https://stewardship.adventist.org/page674
divisions.notes: ECD tithe grew from $9.9M (2003) to $59.3M (2017), representing 1.67% of world tithe. Exact internal split governed by ECD Working Policy, not publicly available. Territory: Burundi, DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Rwanda, Somalia, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda.

divisions.name: Euro-Asia Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Russia, Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia, Armenia, and Central Asian republics. One of the smallest divisions by tithe income. Exact internal split governed by ESD Working Policy.

divisions.name: Inter-American Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available. IAD is one of the largest divisions by membership.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions); https://interamerica.org/2019/10/adventist-church-leaders-approve-plan-for-tithe-parity-among-divisions/
divisions.notes: Territory: Mexico, Central America, Caribbean, Colombia, Venezuela, Guyana, Suriname, French Guiana. Very large membership base but lower per-capita tithe than NAD/SPD/EUD/TED.

divisions.name: Inter-European Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available. European divisions likely have higher conference retention due to higher cost of living and smaller membership base.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Parts of Western and Southern Europe including France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Portugal, Austria, Switzerland, Czech Republic, Slovakia, Romania, Bulgaria. One of the wealthier non-NAD divisions.

divisions.name: Northern Asia-Pacific Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Japan, South Korea, Mongolia, Taiwan, China (Hong Kong, Macau). South Korea contributes significant tithe; Japan has high per-capita giving.

divisions.name: South American Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available. SAD is second-largest division by tithe income after NAD.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Peru, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, Ecuador. Brazil alone has massive Adventist membership. SAD's tithe has grown significantly, now second only to NAD globally.

divisions.name: Southern Africa-Indian Ocean Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: South Africa, Botswana, Lesotho, Mozambique, Namibia, Swaziland, Zimbabwe, Madagascar, Malawi, Zambia, Indian Ocean islands. Delegate Kenaope Kenaope from SID supported tithe parity at 2019 Annual Council.

divisions.name: Southern Asia Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: India (most states), Nepal, Bhutan. Very large membership but low per-capita tithe. Likely receives significant GC appropriations back.

divisions.name: Southern Asia-Pacific Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos, Myanmar, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Pakistan. Philippines is a major Adventist population centre.

divisions.name: Trans-European Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available. TED news confirmed GC is aiming for 3% parity by 2030.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions); https://ted.adventist.org/news/general-conference-spring-meeting/
divisions.notes: Territory: UK, Ireland, Scandinavia, Netherlands, Hungary, Poland, Baltic states, Pakistan, Middle East (some), parts of Africa. Relatively wealthy but small membership. The TED Spring 2025 meeting confirmed ongoing effort toward 3% parity by 2030.

divisions.name: West-Central Africa Division
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 Exact conference/union/division split not publicly available.
divisions.source: GC tithe parity policy (2% to GC for non-NAD divisions)
divisions.notes: Territory: Nigeria, Ghana, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Senegal, Liberia, Sierra Leone, and other West/Central African nations. Nigeria is a major Adventist population centre. Rapidly growing tithe base.

divisions.name: Middle East and North Africa Union Mission
divisions.special_funds: 🔴 MENAUM is attached directly to the GC rather than being a division. It operates as a union mission. Tithe flow differs from standard division model — likely receives significant GC subsidies rather than contributing tithe upward.
divisions.notes: Territory: Middle East, North Africa, parts of Central Asia. Very small Adventist membership. Not a division — it's a GC-attached union mission with different financial arrangements. Tithe flow data is not comparable to divisions.
